channel.title: PatchBrief
channel.description: Short operator-ready briefs on public vulnerability advisories, known exploited vulnerabilities, vendor updates, and threat activity.

item.title: CISA KEV adds actively exploited Chrome V8 type confusion zero-day
item.description: Google confirmed exploitation in the wild for CVE-2024-7971, a type confusion vulnerability in the V8 JavaScript engine. CISA added it to the KEV catalog. All desktop Chrome versions before 128.0.6613.84 are affected.

item.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday: critical RCE in Message Queuing (MSMQ)
item.description: June 2024 Patch Tuesday includes CVE-2024-30080, a critical remote code execution vulnerability in Microsoft Message Queuing. NVD rates this CVSS 9.8. Environments that still expose MSMQ to the network should prioritize installation.

item.title: CISA KEV adds exploited Palo Alto GlobalProtect command injection
item.description: CISA added CVE-2024-3400 to the Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. Unauthenticated command injection in GlobalProtect allows root-level code execution on exposed portal and gateway interfaces. Active exploitation observed in the wild.

item.title: Ransomware groups actively targeting unpatched Fortinet SSL-VPN devices
item.description: Threat reporting indicates ransomware operators are opportunistically scanning for and compromising Fortinet SSL-VPN devices running versions with known authentication bypass issues. Organizations with internet-exposed FortiGate management interfaces should review patch status immediately.

item.title: VMware ESXi heap overflow — ransomware campaigns targeting unpatched hypervisors
item.description: Ransomware operators continue to target ESXi hypervisors through CVE-2021-21974, a heap overflow in the OpenSLP service. Despite the patch being available since 2021, many environments remain exposed. Successful exploitation allows code execution as root.

item.title: ALPHV/BlackCat ransomware targets healthcare sector via unpatched VPN appliances
item.description: CISA and the FBI issued a joint advisory on ALPHV/BlackCat ransomware following healthcare sector intrusions through unpatched VPN appliances. The group has exploited known vulnerabilities in Citrix NetScaler, Cisco ASA, and Fortinet devices as initial access vectors.

item.title: Ivanti Connect Secure authentication bypass and command injection — active exploitation
item.description: Ivanti disclosed CVE-2023-46805 (authentication bypass) and CVE-2024-21887 (command injection) affecting Connect Secure and Policy Secure. When chained, they allow unauthenticated remote code execution. CISA issued an emergency directive requiring federal agencies to act.

item.title: CISA KEV: Cisco IOS XE Web UI privilege escalation — zero-day mass exploitation
item.description: Cisco disclosed CVE-2023-20198, a critical zero-day in IOS XE allowing unauthenticated attackers to create a privilege level 15 account via the Web UI feature. Mass exploitation was observed within hours of disclosure, with thousands of devices implanted with a backdoor.
